title: Bounded No-Install Workstation Pnpm Path Revalidation
objective: Use only existing workstation pnpm capability to rerun the canonical operations-web typecheck and browser smoke commands for the mirrored Packet 031 source artifact, then decide whether publication can open next or whether rollback/defer handling is more truthful.
active_role: Olares Packet 031 Workstation Pnpm Revalidation Operator
dependency_note: Packet 033 confirmed the Packet 031 two-file source artifact remains exact between host and workstation with diff SHA-256 `65882514ad1b609dcf5498ef23fbe18953e8f6e925e0170cb57c044809688f91`. `pnpm` is absent from the active PATH, but an existing user-level shim at `C:/Users/jjswe/AppData/Roaming/npm/pnpm.cmd` runs `pnpm@10.33.2`; the repo declares `packageManager: pnpm@10.0.0` and Corepack last-known-good also names `pnpm@10.0.0`.
required_inputs: Current host and workstation diff hash for the mirrored two-file Packet 031 source artifact
Existing user-level pnpm shim evidence from `C:/Users/jjswe/AppData/Roaming/npm`
Existing workstation operations-web dependencies and Playwright cache only
constrained_outputs: Revalidation evidence for `pnpm --dir "C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform" --filter @apex/operations-web typecheck` using existing workstation capability only
Revalidation evidence for `pnpm --dir "C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform" --filter @apex/operations-web smoke:browser` using existing workstation capability only
A dated handoff stating whether publication can open next or whether rollback/defer handling is the next truthful packet

No publication, host cleanup, source commit, host resync, dependency install, package-manager activation or download, package or lockfile mutation, runtime mutation, service mutation, remote rewrite, force/reset/clean, AI-services rollout, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, migration approval, or old-clone mutation
validation_steps: Reconfirm the workstation and host Packet 031 source artifact diff hash remains `65882514ad1b609dcf5498ef23fbe18953e8f6e925e0170cb57c044809688f91` before running commands.
Confirm `pnpm` remains absent from the active PATH and the existing user-level shim remains present; do not install, activate Corepack, download package managers, or mutate package-manager state.

Use a process-local PATH prepend for `C:/Users/jjswe/AppData/Roaming/npm`, or an equivalent explicit invocation that preserves the canonical repo command semantics, so the authored `pnpm --dir ...` commands can execute without persistent environment changes.
Record pnpm version, command outputs, any generated ignored artifacts, and whether the source artifact remains unchanged after validation.
Decide the single next packet: publication gate only if canonical validation passes, otherwise rollback/defer or blocker handling.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes no database writes and no runtime, service, package, lockfile, install, publication, host-cleanup, remote, AI-services, Gitea, canonical-hosting, migration, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation. It authorizes only no-install workstation command revalidation using existing pnpm capability.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-04. Packet 034 reconfirmed host/workstation source parity at diff SHA-256 `65882514ad1b609dcf5498ef23fbe18953e8f6e925e0170cb57c044809688f91`, used only an existing user-level `pnpm@10.33.2` shim through a process-local PATH prepend, passed canonical `pnpm --dir ... typecheck`, and passed canonical `pnpm --dir ... smoke:browser` with 3 Playwright tests passing. No tracked package or lockfile changes were observed. Publication is now eligible only through a separate bounded gate.
next_action: Open only Packet 035 for bounded Packet 031 source artifact publication and host-mirror resync. Do not include unrelated `.vercelignore`, install, activate or download package managers, mutate runtime or services, change packages or lockfiles, clean the host artifact outside byte-equivalence handling, rewrite remotes, force, reset, clean, mutate the old clone, or approve migration from Packet 034.
